name: Givelight Foundation
mission: Givelight Foundations primary exempt purpose is to provide shelter, clothing and food to children who have been orphaned by a natural disaster or other global crisis; to provide educational, spiritual and social services to the children residing in these orphanages.
theoryOfChange: By providing high-quality education and a loving family environment (feeding the body, mind, and soul), orphans can reach their highest human potential and break the cycle of poverty.
scoreSummary: GiveLight Foundation shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: P&G Humanitarian Award (to founder Dian Alyan) (P&G Alumni)
Gratitude Network Fellow (to founder Dian Alyan) (Gratitude Network)

zakatClaimEvidence: Zakat: Used strictly for essentials like food, clothing, books, and healthcare. More info. ... Zakat can be paid to the following who fall into one or more of eight zakatable categories designated by God in the Quran (9:60). ... Collectors of Zakat (i.e. GiveLight Foundation). (Source: https://givelight.org/donate/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 59% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 135 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 5.9% of revenue ($134,108 on $2,270,848)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 38/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Separate restricted/endowment funds from operating reserves and publish a clear reserve-use policy.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

components.evidence: Board: 4 members, but For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny. — oversight concern
components.name: Governance

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions zakat compliance but doesn't explicitly connect it to the minor risk deductions applied. While the connection is implied, making it explicit would improve clarity.
Extremely high working capital (135.0 months) may indicate hoarding or data error

risks.description: High domestic burn: 59% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
CEO compensation is 5.9% of revenue ($134,108 on $2,270,848)

all_citations.claim: GiveLight Foundation holds a Charity Navigator score of 94.66666666666667/100.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and provides details on zakat policy and asnaf categories.
The organization reported total revenue of $2,270,848 and maintains a program expense ratio of 88.7%.
GiveLight builds loving homes and families for orphans in response to extreme circumstances like natural disasters.
The foundation tracks outcomes such as university degrees attained and the number of orphanage homes operating in countries like Indonesia and Sri Lanka.
GiveLight provides various giving methods and donation options for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 69/100, reflecting a high level of donor alignment and financial health, though impact scores are moderated by the high cost per beneficiary inherent in direct residential care.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing data transparency to improve medium-level data confidence scores.
Expanding third-party impact verification beyond internal annual reporting.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The foundation shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a Zakat-eligible organization that follows specific asnaf categories [2][6]. Its focus on global education and orphan care addresses high-urgency needs with a proven track record of building and operating multiple international homes [4][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: GiveLight demonstrates direct service impact by maintaining a high Charity Navigator score of 94.66666666666667/100 and a program expense ratio of 88.7% [1][3]. While the cost per beneficiary is calculated at $1,377.81, this supports comprehensive care including meals, housing, and university degrees [5].

baseline_narrative.headline: GiveLight Foundation provides a global network of loving homes and educational support for orphans facing extreme circumstances.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 88.7%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised.
Strong financial sustainability with 135.0 months of working capital.
baseline_narrative.summary: GiveLight Foundation is a world-recognized crisis relief organization that builds sustainable homes and families for orphaned children in response to natural disasters and poverty [4]. The organization operates dedicated programs in Indonesia, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh, focusing on long-term stability and university-level educational attainment [5].

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for Muslim donors as the organization is <cite id="2">zakat-eligible</cite> and serves <cite id="2">asnaf categories including the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin)</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The charity could <cite id="2">expand services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust GiveLight due to its <cite id="1">94.67/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite> and its <cite id="3">20-year operating history</cite> since its founding in 2005.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="3">expand its board of directors</cite> to increase independent oversight.

impact.explanation: Operations are highly efficient with a <cite id="1">88.7% program expense ratio</cite> and a focus on <cite id="5">direct service delivery</cite> through orphanages and schools.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> with explicit causal pathways for their educational interventions.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support long-term orphan care and education through a lean, zakat-eligible organization with exceptional financial reserves.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat obligations by helping orphans
Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Prefer supporting the full educational lifecycle of a child
Seek an established organization with a 20-year track record
giving_considerations: The organization has very high reserves, meaning immediate cash needs may be lower than peers
Focus is on long-term stability rather than rapid emergency response
Governance is managed by a small, dedicated team
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth startups or those who require large, independent boards for institutional giving requirements.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects consistent internal tracking of <cite id="5">university degrees and home construction</cite> without external RCTs.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: GiveLight provides a home environment and education to orphans to break the cycle of poverty and create self-sufficient adults.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking educational attainment ensures that the investment in a child's early life leads to actual economic independence.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While reserves are high, the foundation seeks to <cite id="5">build more homes on a global scale</cite>.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: 8 countries including Indonesia, Pakistan, and USA

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 88.7%</cite> according to recent filings, with <cite id="1">fundraising costs of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
GiveLight holds <cite id="1">135.0 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, providing significant long-term sustainability.
The foundation earned a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 Leadership score</cite> and an <cite id="1">94/100 Accountability & Finance score</cite> from Charity Navigator.

strengths_deep_dive: GiveLight Foundation demonstrates a rare level of fiscal discipline for its size. By keeping <cite id="3">officer compensation at $137,082</cite> and minimizing overhead, they ensure that <cite id="3">$2,103,955 in contributions</cite> directly supports their mission. Their <cite id="5">university scholarship program</cite> represents a high-impact intervention, as it transitions orphans into self-sufficient professionals, a metric they track through <cite id="5">university degrees attained</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2005, GiveLight Foundation is a world-recognized crisis relief organization that <cite id="4">builds sustainable homes and families for orphans</cite> in response to natural disasters and extreme poverty. The organization distinguishes itself by moving beyond immediate relief to provide <cite id="5">long-term stability and university-level educational support</cite>. GiveLight maintains a strong financial profile, evidenced by an <cite id="1">overall score of 94.94.66666666666667/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. In the 2023 tax year, the foundation reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $2,186,834</cite>, with a primary focus on direct service delivery. Their operational model is exceptionally lean, maintaining a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, which ensures that donor contributions are maximized for program impact. The foundation currently operates in multiple countries including <cite id="5">Indonesia, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh</cite>, where they manage dedicated orphanages and scholarship funds. By focusing on the full lifecycle of a child's development, from <cite id="5">early childhood care to higher education</cite>, GiveLight aims to break the cycle of poverty for the children in its care.
